int
  utf8_continuation_bytes_needed (unsigned char first_byte)


#include <include/ascii-chat/util/utf8.h>


Determine how many additional bytes are needed to complete a UTF-8
    character.


Parameters


first_byte The first byte of a potential UTF-8
  sequence


Returns


Number of continuation bytes needed (0-3), or -1 if
  invalid start byte


Given the first byte of a UTF-8 sequence, determines how many
    additional bytes are needed to complete the character. Returns 0 for ASCII
    (1-byte chars), 1 for 2-byte sequences, 2 for 3-byte sequences, 3 for 4-byte
    sequences.




Useful in interactive input loops where bytes arrive one at a time
    and you need to know when a complete UTF-8 character has been received.




Example








// Read multi-byte UTF-8 character interactively
unsigned char first_byte = getchar();
int continuation_bytes = utf8_continuation_bytes_needed(first_byte);
if (continuation_bytes < 0) {


    // Invalid start byte


    continue;
}
// Read continuation_bytes more bytes
for (int i = 0; i < continuation_bytes; i++) {


    unsigned char next_byte = getchar();


    // Validate it's a continuation byte (10xxxxxx)


    if ((next_byte & 0xC0) != 0x80) {


        // Invalid continuation byte


        break;


    }
}





Definition at line 222 of file utf8.c.


222                                                              {
223   if ((first_byte & 0x80) == 0) {
224     return 0; // ASCII (0xxxxxxx) - single byte, no continuation needed
225   }
226   if ((first_byte & 0xE0) == 0xC0) {
227     return 1; // 110xxxxx - 2 byte sequence, 1 continuation byte needed
228   }
229   if ((first_byte & 0xF0) == 0xE0) {
230     return 2; // 1110xxxx - 3 byte sequence, 2 continuation bytes needed
231   }
232   if ((first_byte & 0xF8) == 0xF0) {
233     return 3; // 11110xxx - 4 byte sequence, 3 continuation bytes needed
234   }
235   return -1; // Invalid UTF-8 start byte
236 }










int
  utf8_decode (const uint8_t * s, uint32_t * codepoint)


#include <include/ascii-chat/util/utf8.h>


Decode a UTF-8 sequence to a Unicode codepoint.


Parameters


s Pointer to UTF-8 byte sequence (must not be
  NULL)


codepoint Output parameter for decoded codepoint (must not be NULL)


Returns


Number of bytes consumed (1-4), or -1 if invalid
  sequence


Decodes a UTF-8 byte sequence starting at s to a Unicode
    codepoint. Validates the UTF-8 encoding during decoding and returns the
    number of bytes consumed. Returns -1 if the sequence is invalid (overlong
    encoding, invalid bytes, etc.).




Note


Input pointer must point to the start of a valid UTF-8
  sequence.



Output codepoint is set even if sequence is invalid (check return
    value).




Function does not advance the pointer - caller must advance by
    return value.




Return value of -1 indicates invalid UTF-8 sequence.




Example








const uint8_t *str = (uint8_t*)"Hello 世界";
uint32_t cp;
int len;
// Decode 'H' (1 byte)
len = utf8_decode(str, &cp);  // Returns 1, cp = 'H'
str += len;
// Decode '世' (3 bytes)
len = utf8_decode(str, &cp);  // Returns 3, cp = U+4E16
str += len;

22   }

24     // Validate continuation byte
25     if ((s[1] & 0xC0) != 0x80)
26       return -1;
27     *codepoint = (((uint32_t)(s[0] & 0x1F) << 6) | (uint32_t)(s[1] & 0x3F));
28     return 2;
29   } else if ((s[0] & 0xF0) == 0xE0) {
30     // Validate continuation bytes
31     if ((s[1] & 0xC0) != 0x80 || (s[2] & 0xC0) != 0x80)
32       return -1;
33     *codepoint = (((uint32_t)(s[0] & 0x0F) << 12) | ((uint32_t)(s[1] & 0x3F) << 6) | (uint32_t)(s[2] & 0x3F));
34     return 3;
35   } else if ((s[0] & 0xF8) == 0xF0) {
36     // Validate continuation bytes
37     if ((s[1] & 0xC0) != 0x80 || (s[2] & 0xC0) != 0x80 || (s[3] & 0xC0) != 0x80)

41     return 4;
42   }
43   return -1; // Invalid
44 }

int
  utf8_display_width (const char * str)


#include <include/ascii-chat/util/utf8.h>


Calculate terminal display width of a UTF-8 string.


Parameters


str UTF-8 string (must not be NULL)


Returns


Display width in terminal columns (0 on empty or invalid
  input)


Converts UTF-8 byte sequences to Unicode codepoints and calculates
    the terminal display width using utf8proc_charwidth(). Properly handles:


    
  
  • Multi-byte UTF-8 sequences (1-4 bytes per character)
    • 
  
  • Wide characters (CJK, emoji - typically 2 columns)
    • 
  
  • Control characters and combining marks (0 width)
    • 
  
  • Invalid UTF-8 sequences (stops processing)
    • 



Terminal display width differs from byte count:


    
  
  • ASCII 'A' = 1 byte = 1 column
    • 
  
  • 
  
  • 
  
  • 



Note


Uses utf8proc library for accurate Unicode
  character-width computation



Returns 0 for NULL or empty string




Invalid UTF-8 sequences stop processing (partial width
  returned)




Example








int width = utf8_display_width("Hello");      // Returns 5 (5 ASCII chars)
int width = utf8_display_width("Café");       // Returns 4 (é is 1 column)
int width = utf8_display_width("中国");       // Returns 4 (2 CJK chars × 2 columns)
int width = utf8_display_width("😀");         // Returns 2 (emoji is 2 columns)





Definition at line 46 of file utf8.c.


46                                         {
47   if (!str || !*str) {
48     return 0;
49   }
50 
51   int width = 0;
52   const utf8proc_uint8_t *p = (const utf8proc_uint8_t *)str;
53 
54   while (*p) {
55     // Skip ANSI escape sequences (ESC [ ... m)
56     // Pattern: \x1b [ [0-9;]* m
57     if (p[0] == 0x1b && p[1] == '[') {
58       // Found ANSI escape sequence start
59       p += 2; // Skip ESC[
60       // Skip digits and semicolons until we find 'm'
61       while (*p && *p != 'm') {
62         p++;
63       }
64       if (*p == 'm') {
65         p++; // Skip the 'm'
66       }
67       continue;
68     }
69 
70     utf8proc_int32_t codepoint;
71     utf8proc_ssize_t len = utf8proc_iterate(p, -1, &codepoint);
72 
73     if (len <= 0) {
74       // End of string or invalid UTF-8 sequence, stop processing
75       break;
76     }
77 
78     // Get display width of this codepoint
79     int char_width = utf8proc_charwidth(codepoint);
80     if (char_width < 0) {
81       // Control character or unprintable - treat as 0 width
82       char_width = 0;
83     }
84     width += char_width;
85     p += len;
86   }
87 
88   return width;
89 }

int
  utf8_display_width_n (const char * str, size_t max_bytes)


#include <include/ascii-chat/util/utf8.h>


Calculate terminal display width of a UTF-8 string up to byte

Parameters


str UTF-8 string (must not be NULL)


max_bytes Maximum bytes to process


Returns


Display width in terminal columns


Like utf8_display_width() but stops after processing
    max_bytes bytes. Useful for calculating display width of substrings or with
    length-limited buffers.




Note


Stops processing at max_bytes boundary or null
  terminator, whichever comes first



Invalid UTF-8 sequences stop processing (partial width
  returned)




Uses utf8proc library for accurate Unicode character-width
    computation




Example

104       p += 2; // Skip ESC[

116     utf8proc_ssize_t len = utf8proc_iterate(p, end - p, &codepoint);
117 
118     if (len <= 0) {
119       // End of string or invalid UTF-8 sequence
120       break;
121     }
122 
123     // Get display width of this codepoint
124     int char_width = utf8proc_charwidth(codepoint);
125     if (char_width < 0) {
126       // Control character or unprintable - treat as 0 width
127       char_width = 0;
128     }
129     width += char_width;
130     p += len;
131   }
132 
133   return width;
134 }

bool utf8_is_ascii_only (const char * str)


#include <include/ascii-chat/util/utf8.h>


Check if a string contains only ASCII characters.


Parameters


str String to check (must not be NULL)


Returns


true if all characters are ASCII (0x00-0x7F), false if
  any non-ASCII found


Validates UTF-8 encoding and checks that all decoded codepoints
    are ASCII. Useful for security-sensitive strings that should only contain
    ASCII (e.g., session identifiers to prevent homograph attacks).




Returns false if string contains invalid UTF-8 sequences.




Note


Empty string is considered ASCII-only (returns true)



Returns false for NULL input (not validated)




Prevents homograph attack vector (e.g., Cyrillic 'а' vs
    ASCII 'a')




Example








// Verify session string contains only ASCII to prevent homograph spoofing
if (!utf8_is_ascii_only(session_string)) {


    log_warn("Session string contains non-ASCII characters");


    return false;
}





Definition at line 165 of file utf8.c.


165                                          {
166   if (!str) {
167     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "str is NULL");
168     return false;
169   }
170 
171   // Fast path: Check if all bytes are in ASCII range (0x00-0x7F)
172   // ASCII characters are 0x00-0x7F (single byte, high bit clear)
173   // Multi-byte UTF-8 has continuation bytes with high bit set (10xxxxxx pattern)
174   // So if all bytes have high bit clear, it's guaranteed ASCII-only
175   const unsigned char *p = (const unsigned char *)str;
176   while (*p) {
177     if ((*p & ~0x7F) != 0) {
178       return false; // Non-ASCII byte found (high bit set)
179     }
180     p++;
181   }
182   return true;
183 }

#include <include/ascii-chat/util/utf8.h>


Check if a string is valid UTF-8.


Parameters


str String to validate (must not be NULL)


Returns


true if valid UTF-8, false if invalid sequences
  detected


Scans the entire string and validates that all byte sequences
    conform to UTF-8 encoding rules. Returns false immediately on first invalid
    sequence.




Note


Returns true for empty string (valid)



Returns false for NULL input (not validated)




More efficient than decoding all codepoints if only validation is
    needed

    log_error("Invalid UTF-8 in user input");

}

int
  utf8_next_char_bytes (const char * str, size_t max_bytes)


#include <include/ascii-chat/util/utf8.h>


Get byte length of next UTF-8 character.


Parameters


str Pointer to UTF-8 byte sequence (must not be
  NULL)

Returns


Number of bytes in next character (1-4), or -1 if invalid
  UTF-8


Determines how many bytes are needed for the next complete UTF-8
    character. Uses utf8proc for robust UTF-8 handling.




Useful for interactive text input where you need to handle
    multi-byte UTF-8 characters character-by-character (e.g., password
  prompts).




Note


Returns -1 for invalid UTF-8 sequences or if end of
  string reached



Requires utf8proc library which is already a project
  dependency




Example








// Handle multi-byte UTF-8 in password input

// Returns 1 for 'c', 1 for 'a', 1 for 'f', 2 for 'é'

int
  utf8_read_and_insert_continuation_bytes (char * buffer, size_t * cursor,
  size_t * len, size_t max_len, int continuation_bytes, int(* read_byte_fn
  )(void))


#include <include/ascii-chat/util/utf8.h>


Read continuation bytes and insert them into buffer at cursor
    position.


Parameters


buffer Text buffer to insert bytes into


cursor Current cursor position (modified to reflect new position)


len Current buffer length (modified to reflect new length)


max_len Maximum buffer size


continuation_bytes Number of continuation bytes to read


read_byte_fn Callback function to read next byte (should return int like
  getchar)


Returns


0 on success, -1 if EOF or buffer overflow


Reads continuation_bytes bytes from input using the provided
    callback function, shifts existing buffer content right, and inserts each
    byte at the cursor position. Updates cursor and len to reflect the new
    state.




Useful for handling multi-byte UTF-8 input in interactive prompts
    where bytes arrive one at a time (password input, terminal prompts,
  etc).




Example








// In interactive password input
int continuation_bytes = utf8_continuation_bytes_needed((unsigned char)first_byte);
if (continuation_bytes > 0) {


    int result = utf8_read_and_insert_continuation_bytes(


        buffer, &cursor, &len, max_len, continuation_bytes, getchar);


    if (result < 0) {


        // Handle EOF or overflow


    }
}

239                                                                                                {
240   if (!buffer || !cursor || !len || continuation_bytes <= 0 || !read_byte_fn) {
241     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "invalid params");
242     return -1;
243   }
244 
245   for (int i = 0; i < continuation_bytes && *len < max_len - 1; i++) {
246     int next_byte = read_byte_fn();
247     if (next_byte == EOF) {
248       return -1; // EOF reached
249     }
250 
251     // Shift characters right to make room
252     memmove(&buffer[*cursor + 1], &buffer[*cursor], *len - *cursor);
253     buffer[*cursor] = (char)next_byte;
254     (*len)++;
255     (*cursor)++;
256   }
257 
258   return 0;
259 }

const
  char * utf8_strcasestr (const char * haystack, const char * needle)


#include <include/ascii-chat/util/utf8.h>


Case-insensitive substring search with full Unicode support.


Parameters


haystack String to search in (must not be NULL)


needle Substring to search for (must not be NULL)


Returns


Pointer to first occurrence of needle in haystack, or
  NULL if not found


Performs case-insensitive substring search using Unicode case
    folding. Properly handles all Unicode characters including accented letters,
    Greek, Cyrillic, CJK, emoji, and other scripts.




Uses utf8proc library for accurate Unicode case folding according
    to Unicode standard. This ensures correct matching for:


    
  
  • ASCII: "TEST" matches "test"
    • 
  
  • Accented: "CAFÉ" matches "café"
    • 
  
  • Greek:
      "ΕΛΛΗΝΙΚΆ"
      matches
      "ελληνικά"
    • 
  
  • Cyrillic:
      "РУССКИЙ"
      matches
      "русский"
    • 



Note


Returns NULL if needle is empty string



Returns haystack if needle is empty




Case folding follows Unicode standard (more complex than simple
    toupper/tolower)




Uses utf8proc for accurate Unicode handling




Example








const char *found = utf8_strcasestr("Hello WORLD", "world");
// Returns pointer to "WORLD" in the haystack
const char *found2 = utf8_strcasestr("Café français", "FRANÇAIS");
// Returns pointer to "français" (case-insensitive + accents)





Uses utf8proc for Unicode case folding according to Unicode
    standard. This properly handles all Unicode scripts including Greek,
    Cyrillic, accented characters, and more.


Definition at line 272 of file utf8.c.


272                                                                       {
273   if (!haystack || !needle) {
274     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "invalid params");
275     return NULL;
276   }
277 
278   // Empty needle matches at start of haystack
279   if (needle[0] == '\0') {
280     return haystack;
281   }
282 
283   // Get lengths
284   size_t haystack_len = strlen(haystack);
285   size_t needle_len = strlen(needle);
286 
287   if (needle_len > haystack_len) {
288     return NULL;
289   }
290 
291   // Case-fold both strings using utf8proc
292   // UTF8PROC_CASEFOLD performs Unicode case folding
293   // UTF8PROC_STABLE ensures stable output
294   // UTF8PROC_COMPOSE normalizes composed characters
295   utf8proc_option_t options = UTF8PROC_CASEFOLD | UTF8PROC_STABLE | UTF8PROC_COMPOSE;
296 
297   // Case-fold the needle (pattern to search for)
298   utf8proc_uint8_t *needle_folded = NULL;
299   utf8proc_ssize_t needle_folded_len =
300       utf8proc_map((const utf8proc_uint8_t *)needle, (utf8proc_ssize_t)needle_len, &needle_folded, options);
301 
302   if (needle_folded_len < 0 || !needle_folded) {
303     // Invalid UTF-8 in needle
304     if (needle_folded) {
305       free(needle_folded);
306     }
307     return NULL;
308   }
309 
310   // Try each position in haystack
311   const char *haystack_pos = haystack;
312   while (*haystack_pos != '\0') {
313     // Calculate remaining haystack length
314     size_t remaining = haystack_len - (size_t)(haystack_pos - haystack);
315 
316     if (remaining < needle_len) {
317       // Not enough characters left to match
318       break;
319     }
320 
321     // Case-fold the current haystack window
322     utf8proc_uint8_t *haystack_folded = NULL;
323     utf8proc_ssize_t haystack_folded_len =
324         utf8proc_map((const utf8proc_uint8_t *)haystack_pos, (utf8proc_ssize_t)needle_len, &haystack_folded, options);
325 
326     if (haystack_folded_len >= 0 && haystack_folded) {
327       // Compare case-folded strings
328       if ((size_t)haystack_folded_len == (size_t)needle_folded_len &&
329           memcmp(haystack_folded, needle_folded, (size_t)needle_folded_len) == 0) {
330         // Match found!
331         free(haystack_folded);
332         free(needle_folded);
333         return haystack_pos;
334       }
335       free(haystack_folded);
336     }
337 
338     // Move to next UTF-8 character in haystack
339     utf8proc_int32_t codepoint;
340     utf8proc_ssize_t bytes = utf8proc_iterate((const utf8proc_uint8_t *)haystack_pos, -1, &codepoint);
341     if (bytes <= 0) {
342       // Invalid UTF-8, move by one byte
343       haystack_pos++;
344     } else {
345       haystack_pos += bytes;
346     }
347   }
348 
349   free(needle_folded);
350   return NULL;
351 }

size_t
  utf8_to_codepoints (const char * str, uint32_t * out_codepoints, size_t
  max_codepoints)


#include <include/ascii-chat/util/utf8.h>


Convert UTF-8 string to array of Unicode codepoints.


Parameters


str UTF-8 string to convert (must not be NULL)


out_codepoints Output array for decoded codepoints (must not be NULL)


max_codepoints Maximum number of codepoints to decode


Returns


Number of codepoints decoded, or SIZE_MAX if invalid
  UTF-8 detected


Decodes a UTF-8 string into an array of Unicode codepoints (32-bit
    values). Stops when reaching end of string, hitting max_codepoints limit, or
    detecting invalid UTF-8.




Returns SIZE_MAX if invalid UTF-8 sequences are encountered during
    decoding.




Note


Returns 0 if str is NULL, out_codepoints is NULL, or
  max_codepoints is 0



Used by fuzzy matching (Levenshtein), Unicode algorithms, and
    validation




More efficient than calling utf8_decode multiple times in a
  loop




Example








// Compare two strings at codepoint level
uint32_t str1_codepoints[256];
uint32_t str2_codepoints[256];
size_t count1 = utf8_to_codepoints("café", str1_codepoints, 256);
size_t count2 = utf8_to_codepoints("cafe", str2_codepoints, 256);
if (count1 == SIZE_MAX || count2 == SIZE_MAX) {


    return false; // Invalid UTF-8
}
// Now compare at codepoint level instead of bytes
for (size_t i = 0; i < count1 && i < count2; i++) {


    if (str1_codepoints[i] != str2_codepoints[i]) {


        // Different codepoint


    }
}





Definition at line 185 of file utf8.c.


185                                                                                             {
186   if (!str || !out_codepoints || max_codepoints == 0) {
187     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "str is NULL or out_codepoints is NULL or max_codepoints is 0");
188     return 0;
189   }
190 
191   size_t count = 0;
192   const uint8_t *p = (const uint8_t *)str;
193   while (*p && count < max_codepoints) {
194     uint32_t codepoint;
195     int decode_len = utf8_decode(p, &codepoint);
196     if (decode_len < 0) {
197       return -1; // SIZE_MAX - Invalid UTF-8
198     }
199     out_codepoints[count++] = codepoint;
200     p += decode_len;
201   }
202   return count;
203 }

void
  utf8_write_lowercase (FILE * output, const char * text)


#include <include/ascii-chat/util/utf8.h>


Write UTF-8 string to file with ASCII lowercase conversion.


Parameters


output File pointer to write to (must not be NULL)


text UTF-8 string to convert and write (may be NULL)


Writes text to output file, converting ASCII uppercase letters
    (A-Z) to lowercase (a-z) while preserving all UTF-8 multi-byte sequences
    unchanged. This is UTF-8 aware and does not modify non-ASCII characters.




Useful for terminal output where uppercase letters should be
    displayed as lowercase while preserving Unicode characters like emoji and
    accented letters.




Note


If text is NULL, function returns immediately without
  writing anything



Multi-byte UTF-8 sequences (e.g., emoji, CJK characters) are
    copied unchanged




Only ASCII uppercase (A-Z) is converted to lowercase (a-z)




Continuation bytes in multi-byte sequences are never modified




Example








FILE *fp = fopen("output.txt", "w");
// ASCII text: converts uppercase
utf8_write_lowercase(fp, "HELLO World");    // Writes "hello world"
// UTF-8 emoji and non-ASCII: preserved
utf8_write_lowercase(fp, "CAFÉ 😀");       // Writes "café 😀" (emoji unchanged)
// Mixed case with non-ASCII
utf8_write_lowercase(fp, "HELLO 世界");     // Writes "hello 世界" (CJK unchanged)
fclose(fp);





Converts ASCII uppercase to lowercase while preserving all UTF-8
    multi-byte sequences unchanged. This ensures proper handling of emoji and
    non-ASCII characters while still performing ASCII case conversion.


Definition at line 360 of file utf8.c.


360                                                           {
361   if (!text) {
362     return;
363   }
364 
365   for (const unsigned char *p = (const unsigned char *)text; *p; p++) {
366     // Check if this is the start of a multi-byte UTF-8 sequence
367     if (*p >= 0xC0) {
368       // Multi-byte UTF-8 character - copy as-is without modification
369       fputc(*p, output);
370       // Copy continuation bytes (0x80-0xBF)
371       while (p[1] >= 0x80 && p[1] < 0xC0) {
372         fputc(*++p, output);
373       }
374     } else if (*p >= 'A' && *p <= 'Z') {
375       // ASCII uppercase - convert to lowercase
376       fputc(*p + 32, output);
377     } else {
378       // ASCII lowercase or control character - copy as-is
379       fputc(*p, output);
380     }
381   }
382 }
